12 MAY 2026
NOTICE OF ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING
Notice is hereby given that the 2026 Annual General Meeting of the Company will be held at the offices of Aberdeen Group PLC, 1 George Street, Edinburgh EH2 2LL on 10 August 2026 at 10.00am.
The Notice of Annual General Meeting, together with the Annual Report and Financial Statements for the year ended 31 December 2025, have been posted to shareholders.
In accordance with UKLR 6.4.3R,
the Notice of Annual General Meeting, a proxy form and the Annual Report and Accounts have been submitted to the National Storage Mechanism and are available for inspection

CHAIR’S STATEMENT
Background
As longer standing shareholders will be aware from previous communications, the sale in November 2024 of abrdn Property Holdings Limited (aPH) resulted in the disposal of the investment property portfolio barring one asset.
This final holding is the land in the Cairngorms known as Far Ralia, which was originally acquired as part of the Company’s Net Zero Carbon target and is currently being marketed for sale.
There is an update on progress within the Investment Manager’s report.
Following a sale of Far Ralia, and in line with the shareholder vote in May 2024, it is the Board’s intention to progress with a liquidation of the Company and return the proceeds to shareholders.
Review of 2025
As outlined in the 2025 Interim Report & Accounts, the Board and Investment Manager continue to work towards a formal liquidation of the Company, and it remains their sole focus to maximise returns to shareholders and liquidate the Company as soon as practically possible.
During the year and following agreement of the completion accounts in relation to the sale of aPH, the Company returned approximately £15 million to shareholders by way of a Return of Capital (via redeemable bonus shares) and a Final Property Income Distribution.
The Board, in discussion with the Investment Manager, is satisfied that following this distribution the Company retains a prudent level of funds to cover its outgoings for a sufficient period of time to facilitate a liquidation.
The Board remain cognisant that the Company no longer has any income producing assets (excluding interest on cash holdings) and the costs of running the Company are now eroding shareholder funds by approximately £600,000 per annum (net of interest on cash holdings) based on current projections.
The Board and Investment Manager are, therefore, focused on minimising expenditure.
In line with this, the Board have taken the decision to reduce their annual Director’s fees by 10% from 1 April 2026.
Additionally, during the year the Board decided to change the Company’s auditor from Deloitte LLP to Grant Thornton Limited as the latter represented better value for money. This forms part of an ongoing exercise whereby the costs of all the Company's suppliers are being scrutinised in efforts to reduce these where possible.
Potential Delisting
As part of these cost saving efforts, the Board considered the potential for the Company to delist from the London Stock Exchange.
Whilst offering an attractive reduction in costs, it was noted that this could have a material impact on some shareholders.
In

latest.title: Change of Auditor
latest.content:
Today 07:00
abrdn Property Income Trust Limited - Change of Auditor
abrdn Property Income Trust Limited - Change of Auditor
PR Newswire
LONDON, United Kingdom, February 23
ABRDN PROPERTY INCOME TRUST LIMITED Legal Entity Identifier: 549300HHFBWZRKC7RW84
23 February 2026
CHANGE OF AUDITOR
abrdn Property Income Trust Limited (the “Company”) is pleased to announce the appointment of Grant Thornton Limited as the Company's external auditor.
Grant Thornton Limited will conduct the audit of the Company's financial statements for the financial year ending 31 December 2025.
The appointment has been approved by the Board and follows the resignation of Deloitte LLP, the Company's former auditor. Deloitte LLP has provided a statement to the Company confirming that there are no circumstances connected with the change of auditor that need to be brought to the attention of the Company's members or creditors under section 273(2) of The Companies (Guernsey) Law, 2008 (as amended).
The reappointment of Grant Thornton Limited as external auditor will be subject to the approval of shareholders at the 2026 Annual General Meeting.
The Board would like to thank Deloitte LLP for their service and support over the years.
For further information, please contact:
The Company SecretaryNorthern Trust International Fund Administration Services (Guernsey) Limited
PO Box 255
Trafalgar Court
Les Banques
St Peter Port
Guernsey
GY1 3QL
Tel: 01481 745001
END

company_30s.what_they_do: API.L is a very small UK company that owns a handful of properties. It has sold those assets and is now distributing the cash back to shareholders.
company_30s.why_they_matter: It is a classic example of a shell that turns its holdings into a final payout. The company’s story is useful for traders who like to spot the moment a business is being wound down and the stock is forced to a final valuation.
company_30s.current_state: After selling all assets, the company is on a return‑of‑capital schedule. It will hand out 52p per share and then close down. The share price is trading well below that payout level, so the only upside comes from a bounce to the 52p mark.

rns_narrative: The company has sold its property portfolio and is distributing the proceeds. The latest filing announces a 52p capital return to be paid around 24 Dec 2024. There are no plans for new business or growth.
Two themes emerge: (1) a final payout and winding‑up, (2) a lack of future revenue streams. The company is essentially shutting down.
There is no sign of a new catalyst – the only event is the scheduled capital return.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: None – traders see no future beyond the 52p payout.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: The company is a shell with no assets left; after the return it will be delisted or liquidated.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has gone quiet. Most posts focus on the capital return date, with no excitement about a rebound.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – there is no public interest, which is typical for a company that is about to shut down.

near_term_catalysts: The 24 Dec 2024 capital return date – the share price should rally to 52p as cash is paid out.
Any regulatory filing announcing the final liquidation or delisting will likely trigger a sharp drop.
If the company suddenly announces a new investment or property sale, that could be a surprise catalyst.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a spike in volume around 24 Dec and a price move to 52p. Any announcement of delisting, bankruptcy, or a new business line would change the outlook.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is already at the end of its life cycle. The only remaining move is a quick jump to the payout level, after which it will likely collapse or be delisted.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The only upside is a 42p gain if the share is trading at 10p and moves to the 52p payout level. That is a modest 4:1 reward if the price is very low, but the probability of a bounce to that exact level is low once the capital is paid.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Once the 52p payout is made, there is no business left. The share will either be delisted or trade at a fraction of a penny. The worst‑case loss is the entire investment if the company is liquidated without a sale.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: If the share trades at 10p, potential upside is 42p (4×). If it trades at 40p, upside is only 12p against a 40p loss (1:3). The risk side dominates.

playbook.if_bullish: None – no realistic upside beyond the payout.
playbook.if_watching: Set a watch on 24 Dec for the capital return. If the price climbs to 52p and volume spikes, it is a confirmation that the payout is happening.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces delisting or liquidation after the payout, or if the price falls below 5p with no volume, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: API.L is a shell winding down; the only realistic upside is a brief move to the 52p capital return, after which the stock will likely collapse.
